name: Protein Oats & Berries
description: Cooked oats finished with whey and berries. Sets your morning up without spiking you.
ingredients: 300ml skim milk or unsweetened almond milk
instructions: Combine oats and milk in a saucepan. Bring to a low simmer.
Cook 4-5 minutes, stirring, until thick.
Pull off heat. Let it cool 2 minutes (whey will clump in boiling liquid).
Stir through whey, cinnamon, and almond butter if using.
Top with berries. Eat.

name: Three-Egg Veg Scramble on Toast
description: Three eggs, spinach, tomato, on sourdough. Quick, filling, no faffing.
instructions: Whisk eggs in a bowl with salt and pepper.
Toast the sourdough.
Melt butter in a non-stick pan on medium-low.
Add tomatoes, cook 1 minute until softening.
Add spinach, stir until wilted, about 30 seconds.
Pour in eggs. Gentle folds with a spatula. Pull off heat when still a touch wet, they finish cooking on the plate.
Serve on the toast with chilli flakes over the top.

name: Greek Yoghurt Bowl with Granola
description: High-protein yoghurt, granola, banana, honey. Zero cooking.
instructions: Spoon yoghurt into a bowl.
Top with banana, granola, chia, and a drizzle of honey.
Done.

name: Cottage Cheese & Banana Pancakes
description: Blender pancakes. Three ingredients in the batter, high protein, no sugar crash.
instructions: Blend cottage cheese, banana, eggs, oats, baking powder and vanilla until smooth.
Heat a non-stick pan on medium-low. Spray with oil.
Pour batter to form small pancakes (you'll get 4-5).
Cook 2 minutes per side until golden and set.
Serve with maple syrup.

name: Smoked Salmon Bagel
description: Smoked salmon, light cream cheese, capers, red onion. Classic, fast, no compromise.
instructions: Toast the bagel halves.
Spread cream cheese on both halves.
Layer salmon, red onion, capers, dill, lemon, and a crack of black pepper.
Close and eat, or eat open.

name: Overnight Oats with Peanut Butter
description: Build it the night before. Five minutes the night before, zero minutes in the morning.
instructions: Mix everything in a jar or container.
Lid on. Fridge overnight (minimum 4 hours).
Eat cold straight from the jar.

name: Steak & Eggs
description: Old-school breakfast for a reason. Lean steak, two eggs, butter. That's the meal.
instructions: Pull steak from fridge 20 minutes before cooking. Season heavy with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a pan on high. Sear steak 2-3 minutes each side for medium-rare.
Rest steak on a plate.
In the same pan, drop heat to medium, add butter, crack the eggs in.
Cook eggs sunny-side or to your liking.
Slice steak across the grain, plate with eggs. Optional squeeze of lemon.

name: High-Protein French Toast
description: Egg-soaked wholemeal bread, vanilla, whey. Tastes indulgent. Macros say otherwise.
instructions: Whisk eggs, milk, whey, cinnamon, vanilla in a wide bowl.
Heat a non-stick pan, medium heat, spray with oil.
Dunk each bread slice, soak 10 seconds per side.
Cook 2 minutes per side until golden.
Plate with berries and maple syrup.

name: Veggie & Feta Egg Muffins
description: Batch cook on Sunday, eat all week. Three muffins per serve.
instructions: Oven to 180°C / 350°F. Spray a muffin tin.
Whisk eggs with salt, pepper, oregano.
Divide veg and feta between three muffin cups.
Pour egg mix over the top.
Bake 16-18 minutes until set and lightly golden.
Cool. Store in fridge up to 4 days. Reheat 30 seconds in microwave.

name: Avocado Egg Toast
description: Half an avocado, two eggs, sourdough. Not a brunch-cafe price tag.
instructions: Toast the sourdough.
Mash avocado with lemon, salt, pepper. Spread on toast.
Bring a pot of water to a gentle simmer, add vinegar.
Crack eggs in one at a time, poach 3 minutes for runny yolks.
Lift out with a slotted spoon, drain on paper towel.
Place eggs on top of the avocado toast. Chilli flakes, more pepper. Eat.

name: Garlic Butter Steak & Cajun Mac
description: Cajun-spiced creamy mac and pan-seared steak. Carbs, protein, fat, all dialled.
ingredients: 1 tsp each salt, black pepper, garlic powder, paprika, oregano, thyme (Cajun base, split)
instructions: Cube steak. Toss with half the Cajun spice mix, parsley, oil. Rest 10 minutes.
Boil pasta until al dente. Drain.
Heat pan medium-high, melt half the butter. Sear steak in batches, 3 minutes per side.
Drop heat to low. Add remaining butter, minced garlic, fresh parsley. Toss steak in the garlic butter. Set aside.
Same pan, low: milk, cream cheese, both cheeses, remaining Cajun spice. Stir until smooth.
Fold in pasta. Coat well.
Plate mac, steak on top, split into two.

name: Creamy Honey Garlic Steak Noodles
description: Steak strips in a sticky honey garlic cream sauce over egg noodles.
ingredients: Spring onions and sesame seeds to finish
instructions: Slice steak thin. Toss with salt, pepper, garlic powder, oil.
Boil noodles per packet. Drain.
Heat pan high. Sear beef in batches 1-2 minutes. Remove.
Drop heat to low. Butter, minced garlic, 1 minute.
Add honey, soy sauce, evaporated milk, cream cheese. Stir smooth.
Return beef to the pan. Fold in noodles. Coat.
Finish with spring onion and sesame. Two serves.

name: Korean BBQ Beef Bowls
description: Sweet-savoury sticky Korean beef on rice with sesame and spring onion.
instructions: Cook rice.
Mix the marinade: both soy sauces, honey, garlic, gochujang.
Heat sesame oil in a pan, medium-high. Brown the mince 6-7 minutes, breaking it up.
Drop heat to medium. Pour in marinade. Add sesame seeds and most of the spring onion.
Stir 4-5 minutes until glossy and slightly sticky.
Plate over rice. Remaining spring onion on top. Kimchi if using. Two.

name: Garlic Parmesan Steak Alfredo
description: Steak strips in a parmesan cream sauce on fettuccine.
instructions: Slice beef thin. Toss with salt, pepper, garlic powder, Italian herbs, oil.
Boil pasta to al dente. Drain (keep a small cup of pasta water).
Heat pan high. Sear beef in batches 1-2 min. Set aside.
Drop heat, butter, garlic, 1 minute.
Evaporated milk, cream cheese, parmesan. Stir smooth.
Fold in pasta. Loosen with pasta water if tight.
Plate, steak on top, parsley, pepper. Two.

name: Mongolian Beef Noodles
description: Sweet-savoury sticky beef on egg noodles with spring onion.
ingredients: Sauce: 30g dark soy, 20g light soy, 20g honey, 1.5 tbsp minced garlic, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tsp chilli flakes
instructions: Toss beef with pepper, garlic powder, cornstarch, light soy.
Boil noodles. Drain.
Sauce: mix soys, honey, garlic, ginger, chilli.
Heat oil in a pan, high. Sear beef in batches 1-2 min. Set aside.
Drop heat to medium-low. Pour in sauce, simmer 1-2 min until syrupy.
Return beef. Add spring onion. Toss 1 minute.
Fold in noodles. Sesame over. Two.

name: Garlic Shrimp Fried Rice
description: Quick fried rice with garlic butter prawns. 25-minute dinner.
instructions: Toss prawns with salt, pepper, paprika.
Heat butter in a pan, medium-high. Add half the garlic, 30 sec.
Add prawns, cook 2-3 min until pink. Remove.
Same pan: scramble eggs, break up, remove.
Pan back to high. Bit of oil, remaining garlic, 30 sec.
Add rice, peas, carrot. Stir-fry 3 min.
Add both soy sauces and sesame oil. Toss.
Return prawns, eggs, spring onion. Toss. Two serves.

name: Cottage Cheese & Pineapple
description: Old-school fitness snack. Protein, carbs, no prep.
instructions: Spoon cottage cheese into a bowl.
Top with pineapple. Eat.

name: Greek Yoghurt with Berries & Honey
description: 20g protein for under 200 calories. No excuses.
instructions: Yoghurt in a bowl. Berries and honey on top. Done.

name: Tuna & Crackers
description: Tin of tuna, light mayo, wholegrain crackers. Forever cheap.
instructions: Drain tuna. Mash with mayo, lemon, pepper.
Eat with crackers.

name: Protein Shake & Banana
description: Post-training default. Shake plus a banana.
instructions: Shake whey and milk in a shaker.
Eat the banana with it.

name: Hard-Boiled Eggs & Apple
description: Two eggs, an apple. Boil six on Sunday, snack all week.
instructions: Eggs into cold water, bring to boil, simmer 8-9 minutes. Cold water bath.
Peel, season. Eat with the apple.

name: Edamame with Sea Salt
description: Steamed edamame, salt. That's the whole brief.
instructions: Bring water to boil, drop edamame in, 5 min.
Drain, salt heavy. Eat.

name: Beef Jerky & Cheese
description: Travel snack. Throw it in your bag.
instructions: Unwrap. Eat.

name: Hummus & Veggie Sticks
description: Carrot, capsicum, cucumber, hummus. Beats anything in a vending machine.
instructions: Cut veg into sticks.
Dip and eat.

name: Almonds & Dark Chocolate
description: Handful of almonds, a square of dark chocolate. Works.
instructions: Eat together. Mind the portion, easy to overdo nuts.

name: Protein Rice Cakes
description: Rice cakes, peanut butter, banana. Three minutes, decent macros.
instructions: Spread peanut butter on rice cakes.
Top with banana slices and cinnamon.

name: Crispy Potatoes & Creamy Eggs
description: Hash-style crispy potatoes under soft-scrambled eggs. Diner breakfast at home.
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh chives or spring onion
instructions: Oven 210°C / 410°F. Toss potatoes with paprika, garlic powder, salt, oil. Tray-bake 18-22 minutes until golden.
Whisk eggs with cream cheese, salt, pepper.
Heat butter in a non-stick pan on medium-low.
Pour in eggs. Stir slowly with a spatula in big folds. Pull off the heat when still slightly wet, they finish on the plate.
Plate potatoes, eggs on top, chives over.

name: Creamy Garlic Beef Pasta
description: Lean beef mince in a garlic-parmesan cream sauce, tossed through penne. Simple, fast, hits the numbers.
instructions: Boil pasta until al dente. Save 60ml pasta water, drain.
Heat pan medium-high, melt butter. Brown the mince 6-7 min with salt, pepper, Italian herbs.
Drop heat to low. Add garlic, 1 minute.
Add milk, cream cheese, parmesan. Stir smooth.
Fold in pasta. Loosen with pasta water if it tightens.
Parsley over. Two.

name: Teriyaki Beef Fried Rice
description: Sweet teriyaki beef mince through fried rice. 25 minutes from start to bowl.
ingredients: Teriyaki: 25g light soy sauce, 15g dark soy sauce, 18g honey, 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tsp sesame oil
instructions: Mix teriyaki sauce: both soys, honey, garlic, ginger, sesame oil.
Heat half the oil in a wok or large pan, high. Brown mince 6 min with salt, pepper.
Pour over teriyaki, simmer 2 min until glossy. Remove.
Same pan, drop heat: scramble eggs, break up, remove.
Heat remaining oil, high. Add rice, peas, carrot, half the spring onion. Stir-fry 3 min.
Return beef and eggs. Toss.
Sesame seeds and remaining spring onion over. Two.

name: Chilli Peanut Chicken Noodles
description: Spicy peanut sauce over egg noodles with seared chicken thigh. Big flavour, fast cook.
ingredients: Sauce: 30g peanut butter, 20g light soy sauce, 15g honey, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1 tsp sriracha, 1 tsp chilli flakes, 1 tsp sesame oil, 60ml water
instructions: Toss chicken with salt, paprika, garlic powder, oil.
Boil noodles per packet. Drain.
Sauce: whisk peanut butter, soy, honey, ginger, garlic, sriracha, chilli flakes, sesame oil, water. Smooth.
Heat a pan medium-high. Cook chicken 6-8 min until golden. Remove.
Same pan, low: pour in sauce, simmer 2 minutes until slightly thick.
Return chicken, toss. Add noodles, toss to coat.
Spring onion, peanuts, coriander over. Two.

name: Black Pepper Beef Fried Rice
description: Peppery beef strips through garlicky fried rice. Quick wok-style dinner.
instructions: Toss beef with black pepper, light soy, cornstarch.
Heat half the oil in a wok or large pan, high. Sear beef in batches 1-2 min. Remove.
Same pan, drop heat: scramble eggs, break up, remove.
Pan back to high. Remaining oil, garlic, 30 seconds.
Add rice. Stir-fry 3 minutes.
Pour in dark soy, oyster sauce, sesame oil. Toss.
Return beef and eggs. Add spring onion. Toss.
Sesame seeds over. Two.

name: Crispy Spicy Beef Noodles
description: Crispy-fried beef strips in a chilli-garlic-soy glaze over egg noodles.
ingredients: Sauce: 30g light soy sauce, 15g dark soy sauce, 18g honey, 2 tbsp minced garlic, 1.5 tsp chilli flakes, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp sesame oil
instructions: Toss beef with salt, pepper, garlic powder, cornstarch (gives the crispy edges).
Boil noodles. Drain.
Mix the sauce in a small bowl.
Heat oil in a pan or wok, high. Sear beef in single layer, don't overcrowd, 2-3 min per batch until crispy.
Drop heat to medium-low. Pour in sauce, simmer 1 minute.
Return all the beef, add spring onion. Toss.
Add noodles, toss to coat. Sesame over. Two.

name: Lemon Garlic Prawn Linguine
description: Garlic prawns in a light lemon cream over linguine. 20 minutes flat.
instructions: Toss prawns with salt, pepper, paprika.
Boil pasta until al dente. Reserve 60ml pasta water, drain.
Heat butter in a pan, medium-high. Add half the garlic, 30 seconds.
Add prawns, cook 2-3 min per side until pink. Remove.
Drop heat. Remaining garlic, chilli flakes, lemon zest, 30 seconds.
Add lemon juice, evaporated milk, cream cheese, parmesan. Stir smooth.
Return prawns. Fold in linguine. Loosen with pasta water.
Parsley over. Two.

name: Steak & Eggs with Garlic Butter Toast
description: Lean steak, two eggs, and a slice of garlic butter sourdough. The big-meal breakfast.
instructions: Pull steak out of fridge 20 min before. Season heavy with salt, pepper, garlic powder. Rub oil over.
Toast the sourdough.
Heat pan on high. Sear steak 2-3 min per side for medium-rare. Rest on a plate.
Drop heat to medium. Melt half the butter, add minced garlic, swirl 30 seconds.
Brush half the garlic butter onto the toast. The other half stays in the pan.
Crack the eggs into the same pan. Sunny-side or basted with the garlic butter, your call.
Slice steak across the grain. Plate with eggs, garlic toast, parsley over. One serve.

name: Crispy Orange Chicken Noodles
description: Crispy chicken thigh in a sweet-sticky orange glaze over egg noodles. Better than the takeaway.
ingredients: Sauce: juice and zest of 1 orange, 20g dark soy sauce, 18g honey, 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tsp chilli flakes, 1 tsp rice vinegar
instructions: Toss chicken with salt, pepper, garlic powder, cornstarch.
Boil noodles. Drain.
Heat oil in pan, medium-high. Cook chicken 6-8 min until crispy and golden. Remove.
Same pan, low: pour in sauce. Simmer 2-3 min until syrupy.
Return chicken, toss to glaze. Add noodles, toss to coat.
Spring onion and sesame over. Two.

name: Chicken Chow Mein
description: Classic takeaway noodles. Chicken, cabbage, carrot, soy. Faster than ordering it.
ingredients: Sauce: 25g light soy sauce, 15g dark soy sauce, 10g oyster sauce, 12g honey, 1 tsp sesame oil, 60ml water
instructions: Toss chicken with salt, pepper, soy, cornstarch.
Boil noodles. Drain.
Mix the sauce.
Heat half the oil in a wok or large pan, high. Cook chicken 4-5 min until cooked. Remove.
Same pan, high: remaining oil, garlic, ginger, 30 seconds.
Add cabbage and carrot. Stir-fry 3 minutes.
Return chicken. Add noodles. Pour over sauce. Toss.
Spring onion and sesame over. Two.

name: Chipotle Steak Bowls
description: Smoky-spiced steak with lime-coriander rice, black beans, and a fresh corn salsa.
ingredients: 100g canned black beans, drained and rinsed
Corn salsa: 60g corn kernels, 50g tomato diced, 30g red onion diced, fresh coriander, juice of 1/4 lime, salt
instructions: Cook rice. Once done, fold through lime juice, coriander, salt.
Marinate steak: salt, pepper, cumin, smoked paprika, chipotle paste, lime, oil. 10 min.
Corn salsa: combine corn, tomato, onion, coriander, lime, salt. Set aside.
Heat butter in a pan, medium-high. Sear steak in batches 2-3 min per side.
Plate rice, steak, beans, salsa. Two.

name: Korean Beef Fried Rice
description: Gochujang beef stir-fried through rice with egg and sesame. Sweet-spicy comfort.
ingredients: Sauce: 20g gochujang, 25g light soy sauce, 18g honey, 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1 tsp sesame oil
instructions: Mix sauce ingredients.
Heat half the oil in a wok, high. Brown the mince 6 min with salt and pepper.
Pour in sauce, stir 2 min until glossy. Remove.
Drop heat: scramble eggs, break up, remove.
Pan back to high. Remaining oil, rice, peas, half spring onion. Stir-fry 3 minutes.
Return beef, eggs. Toss.
Sesame and remaining spring onion over. Two.

name: Beef Bulgogi Kimchi Fried Rice
description: Bulgogi-spiced beef through kimchi fried rice. Tangy, spicy, gut-friendly.
ingredients: 400g lean topside, thinly sliced (or 95% lean mince)
Bulgogi marinade: 25g light soy sauce, 20g dark soy sauce, 12g honey, 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tsp sesame oil
120g kimchi, chopped (plus a bit of the juice)
instructions: Toss beef with bulgogi marinade. 10 min.
Heat half the oil in a wok, high. Cook beef 2-3 min if sliced or 6 min if mince. Remove.
Same pan, drop heat: scramble eggs, break up, remove.
Pan back on high. Remaining oil. Add rice and kimchi (plus a splash of kimchi juice). Stir-fry 3 minutes.
Return beef and eggs. Toss.
Spring onion and sesame over. Two.

name: Nacho Cheese Beef Rice
description: Jalapeño-spiked beef on rice with a proper nacho cheese sauce. Big-bowl comfort.
ingredients: Taco spice: 1 tsp each salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, oregano, cumin; 1/2 tsp chilli powder
Cheese sauce: 100ml light evaporated milk, 50g light cream cheese, 30g light cheddar grated, 1 tbsp jalapeño juice (optional)
instructions: Cook rice.
Heat pan medium-high. Melt butter, brown the mince 6 min with taco spice and tomato paste.
Stir in jalapeños, cook 2 minutes. Remove.
Same pan, low: evaporated milk, cream cheese, cheddar, jalapeño juice. Stir smooth.
Plate rice, beef on top, cheese sauce drizzled, coriander, spring onion. Two.

name: Breakfast Burrito
description: Wrapped scrambled eggs, black beans, cheese, and salsa. Hand-held, freezer-friendly if you make a batch.
instructions: Warm the tortilla in a dry pan or microwave 15 seconds. Set aside.
Whisk eggs with salt, pepper, cumin.
Heat butter in a non-stick pan, medium-low. Pour in eggs, stir in soft folds until just set.
Add black beans to warm through, 30 seconds.
Lay tortilla flat. Spoon eggs and beans down the centre. Top with cheese, salsa, yoghurt, coriander.
Fold sides in, roll up tight. Eat hot, or wrap in foil for grab-and-go.

name: Breakfast Quesadilla
description: Crispy tortilla filled with scrambled eggs, melted cheese, and a hit of chilli. Five minutes.
instructions: Whisk eggs with salt, pepper, paprika.
Heat half the butter in a pan, medium-low. Add capsicum, 1 min.
Pour in eggs, stir until just set. Stir through spring onion. Remove.
Wipe the pan. Place one tortilla in, low heat. Sprinkle half the cheese, spread the eggs, remaining cheese, top with second tortilla.
Cook 2 min until golden underneath. Flip with a spatula, 2 min the other side.
Cut into wedges. Hot sauce on the side.

name: Smashed Avocado Toast with Egg
description: Avocado, fried egg, sourdough. The classic that won't quit.
instructions: Toast the sourdough.
Mash avocado with lemon, salt, pepper.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan, medium. Fry eggs to your liking, usually 3 minutes for runny.
Spread avocado on toast. Top with eggs, chilli flakes, feta if using.

name: Egg & Bacon English Muffin
description: Diner-style breakfast sandwich. Crispy bacon, fried egg, sharp cheese on a toasted muffin.
ingredients: Optional: 1 tsp brown sauce or hot sauce
instructions: Toast the English muffin halves.
Heat pan medium-high. Fry bacon 2 minutes per side until crispy. Set aside on paper towel.
Drop heat. Same pan: butter, then crack the egg in. Cook 2 minutes for runny yolk, season.
Build: bottom muffin, cheese, bacon, egg, sauce if using, top muffin. Squeeze together.

name: Sweet Potato Breakfast Hash
description: Spiced sweet potato cubes with peppers and a fried egg on top.
instructions: Heat oil in a pan, medium-high. Add sweet potato, 8-10 min, stirring, until golden and tender.
Add capsicum and onion, 4 min.
Stir through smoked paprika, cumin, salt. 1 min.
Make two wells in the hash. Add butter, crack eggs into the wells. Lid on, 3 min for runny yolks.
Herbs over, hot sauce on the side.

name: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato-pepper sauce with feta. Eat with bread for dipping.
instructions: Heat oil in a small pan, medium. Onion and capsicum, 5 min until soft.
Add garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, chilli flakes, 1 min.
Add tomato paste, 1 min. Pour in chopped tomatoes, simmer 5 min until thickened. Season.
Make two wells in the sauce. Crack eggs in. Lid on, low heat, 4-5 min for runny yolks.
Crumble feta over. Parsley.
Toast the sourdough. Serve alongside for dipping.

name: Banana Protein Pancakes
description: Blender pancakes, protein powder, oats, banana, egg. 35+g protein.
instructions: Blend banana, eggs, oats, whey, baking powder, cinnamon until smooth.
Heat a non-stick pan medium-low. Spray with oil.
Pour batter to form 4-5 small pancakes.
Cook 2 min per side until golden.
Plate with berries and maple syrup.

name: Tofu Scramble on Toast
description: Crumbled spiced tofu with spinach and peppers on sourdough. Vegan, high protein.
ingredients: 1 tsp nutritional yeast (optional, for cheesy flavour)
instructions: Crumble tofu with your hands into rough chunks.
Heat oil in a pan, medium. Add tofu, turmeric, smoked paprika, garlic powder, nutritional yeast, salt. Cook 4 min, stirring.
Toast the sourdough.
Add tomatoes to the pan, 2 min.
Add spinach, wilt 30 seconds.
Plate on toast. Pepper, chilli flakes.

name: Veggie Frittata
description: Oven-baked egg, peppers, spinach, feta. Cook one, eat two days.
instructions: Heat oil in an oven-safe pan, medium. Onion and capsicum, 4 min.
Spinach in, wilt 1 min. Spread evenly.
Whisk eggs with milk, salt, pepper, oregano. Pour over the veg.
Crumble feta over the top.
Pan into the oven, 12-15 min until just set in the middle.
Parsley over. Slice into two.

name: Blueberry Baked Oats
description: Blender baked oats, like a single-serve cake but it's actually breakfast. Bakeable in bulk.
instructions: Oven 180°C / 360°F. Spray a ramekin or small oven dish.
Blend everything except blueberries until smooth.
Fold in blueberries.
Pour into the dish. Bake 20-22 min until set and slightly golden.
Eat warm, or cool and refrigerate for up to 3 days.

name: Chia Pudding Bowl
description: Make the night before, eat in the morning. High fibre, high protein.
instructions: Whisk chia, milk, whey, honey, vanilla in a jar.
Cover. Fridge minimum 4 hours, ideally overnight.
In the morning: top with yoghurt, fruit, almonds. Eat.

name: Berry Protein Smoothie Bowl
description: Thick smoothie eaten with a spoon. Berries, banana, whey, granola on top.
instructions: Blend frozen berries, banana, whey, milk, yoghurt until thick and spoonable. Add less milk for thicker, more for looser.
Pour into a bowl.
Top with granola, chia, fresh berries.

name: Korean Marinated Eggs on Rice
description: Mayak gyeran. Soft-boiled eggs marinated in soy-sesame-chilli, served on hot rice.
ingredients: Marinade: 30g light soy sauce, 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp honey, 1 tsp chilli flakes, 1 tsp sesame seeds, 20g spring onion sliced, 30ml water
instructions: Boil eggs 6.5 minutes for jammy yolks. Cold water bath. Peel.
Mix marinade in a jar or container that fits the eggs.
Add eggs to marinade. Cover. Fridge 1-2 hours minimum (overnight = better, will keep 3 days).
To serve: heat rice. Halve eggs over rice. Spoon over a couple of tablespoons of marinade.
Sesame and spring onion over.

name: Savoury Cottage Cheese Bowl
description: Cottage cheese, cherry tomato, cucumber, avocado, everything seasoning. Two minutes, 28g protein.
ingredients: 1 tsp everything bagel seasoning (or sesame seeds + salt + onion powder)
instructions: Cottage cheese in a bowl.
Top with tomato, cucumber, avocado.
Drizzle oil, lemon. Sprinkle seasoning, pepper. Eat.

name: PB&J Protein Wrap
description: Wrap with peanut butter, low-sugar jam, banana, and a layer of cottage cheese for the protein bump.
instructions: Spread cottage cheese across the wrap.
Drizzle peanut butter and jam.
Lay banana slices down the centre.
Roll up tight. Cut in half if you want.

name: Bagel & Cream Cheese with Lox
description: Classic NYC breakfast, smoked salmon, light cream cheese, capers, red onion on a toasted bagel.
instructions: Toast the bagel halves.
Cream cheese on both halves.
Layer salmon, onion, capers, dill, lemon, pepper.
Eat open-faced (cleaner) or close it up.

name: Breakfast Tacos
description: Three soft tortillas filled with scrambled eggs, avocado, salsa, cheese. Tex-Mex morning.
ingredients: 3 small corn or flour tortillas (40g total)
instructions: Warm tortillas in a dry pan, 20 seconds each side. Stack and cover.
Whisk eggs with salt and pepper.
Melt butter in the pan, low. Pour in eggs, soft scramble.
Divide eggs between tortillas. Top with cheese (melts on the hot eggs), avocado, salsa, spring onion, coriander, hot sauce.

name: Sausage Breakfast Bowl
description: Lean chicken or turkey sausage with sweet potato, eggs, and spinach. Hearty bowl breakfast.
ingredients: 1 tsp hot sauce or 1 tbsp salsa
instructions: Oven 200°C / 400°F. Toss sweet potato with smoked paprika, salt, oil. Tray-bake 18 min.
Last 10 min: add sausages to the same tray, flipping halfway.
Meanwhile, heat butter in a pan, medium. Wilt spinach 1 minute, push to one side.
Crack eggs into the pan, fry to your liking.
Slice sausages. Build bowl: sweet potato, spinach, sausage, eggs. Hot sauce or salsa.

name: High-Protein Yoghurt Parfait
description: Layered Greek yoghurt, whey, berries, granola. Looks fancy in a jar, 35g protein, no cook.
instructions: Whisk whey into yoghurt until smooth.
In a jar or bowl: half yoghurt, half berries, half granola.
Repeat layers.
Drizzle honey, sprinkle chia. Eat.

name: Protein Waffles
description: Whey-and-oat waffle batter. Needs a waffle iron, or use the batter as pancakes if you don't have one.
instructions: Blend oats, whey, egg, milk, baking powder, vanilla, cinnamon until smooth.
Rest the batter 5 min so the oats absorb (this is the trick for fluffiness).
Heat a waffle iron, spray with oil.
Pour in batter (makes 1-2 waffles depending on size). Cook per the iron's settings, usually 4 min.
Plate with berries and syrup.

name: Teriyaki Salmon Rice Bowl
description: Glazed salmon on jasmine rice with edamame and a quick cucumber pickle.
ingredients: Teriyaki: 25g light soy sauce, 15g dark soy sauce, 18g honey, 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tsp sesame oil
Quick cucumber: 100g cucumber sliced, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp soy, 1 tsp sesame seeds
Spring onion, sesame seeds to finish
instructions: Cook rice. Edamame: boil 4 min, drain, salt.
Pickle cucumber: combine, set aside.
Mix teriyaki sauce.
Pat salmon dry, season with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a pan, medium-high. Salmon skin-down, 4 min undisturbed. Flip, 2 more min.
Drop heat. Pour in teriyaki, spoon over salmon to glaze, 1-2 min.
Plate rice, salmon, edamame, cucumber. Sesame, spring onion. Two.

name: Honey Garlic Salmon & Greens
description: Sweet garlic-glazed salmon with roasted broccolini and lemon. Lower-carb, big flavour.
instructions: Oven 200°C / 400°F. Toss broccolini with oil and salt, tray-bake 12 min.
Pat salmon dry, season with salt, pepper, paprika.
Heat oil in an oven-safe pan, medium-high. Sear salmon skin-down 4 min undisturbed. Flip 1 min.
Drop heat. Add butter, garlic, honey, soy, lemon, chilli flakes. Spoon over salmon to glaze, 2 min.
Plate broccolini with salmon, sauce spooned over. Two.

name: Cajun Fish Tacos
description: Spiced white fish in soft tortillas with cabbage slaw and chipotle yoghurt. Fresh, fast, bright.
ingredients: 300g white fish (cod, basa, or barramundi), cubed
Cajun: 1 tsp each salt, pepper, garlic powder, paprika, oregano; 1/2 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp cumin
4 small flour or corn tortillas (~40g each)
Slaw: 100g cabbage thinly sliced, 60g carrot grated, 1 tbsp fresh coriander, 30g 0% Greek yoghurt, 1 tsp olive oil, juice of 1/2 lime, salt, pepper
Sauce: 50g 0% Greek yoghurt, 15g chipotle paste, 1 tsp lime juice
instructions: Slaw: combine cabbage, carrot, coriander, yoghurt, oil, lime, salt, pepper. Chill.
Sauce: whisk yoghurt, chipotle, lime.
Toss fish with Cajun spice, oil, lime juice.
Heat a pan medium-high. Cook fish 2-3 min per side until just cooked through.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan, 15 sec each side.
Build tacos: tortilla, slaw, fish, sauce drizzle, coriander. Lime wedge on the side. Two each.

name: Lemon Dill Prawn Rice Bowl
description: Garlic prawns over lemon rice with a herby yoghurt sauce and crunchy cucumber.
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh dill, chopped (or 1 tsp dried)
Yoghurt sauce: 80g 0% Greek yoghurt, 1 tbsp dill, juice of 1/4 lemon, 1 tsp garlic powder, salt and pepper
instructions: Cook rice. Once done, fold through lemon zest, half the lemon juice, dill, salt.
Yoghurt sauce: mix everything.
Toss prawns with salt, pepper, paprika.
Heat butter in a pan, medium-high. Add garlic, 30 sec.
Add prawns, 2-3 min per side until pink. Finish with remaining lemon juice.
Plate rice, prawns, cucumber, tomato. Sauce drizzled over. Two.

name: Halloumi & Couscous Bowl
description: Pan-seared halloumi over lemon couscous with cherry tomato, cucumber, and tahini-yoghurt dressing.
ingredients: Dressing: 50g 0% Greek yoghurt, 15g tahini, juice of 1/2 lemon, 1 tsp minced garlic, salt, water to loosen
instructions: Couscous: place in a bowl, pour over hot stock, lid on, 5 min. Fluff with a fork. Stir through butter, lemon zest, salt.
Combine tomato, cucumber, onion, herbs.
Dressing: whisk yoghurt, tahini, lemon, garlic, salt. Loosen with water until pourable.
Pat halloumi dry. Rub with smoked paprika.
Heat oil in a pan, medium-high. Sear halloumi 1.5-2 min per side until golden.
Build bowls: couscous base, salad, halloumi on top. Dressing drizzled, pomegranate. Two.

name: Mapo Tofu
description: Silky tofu in a numbing-spicy Sichuan sauce on rice. Cheap, fast, fiery.
ingredients: Sauce: 25g doubanjiang (chilli bean paste, or 15g gochujang + 1 tsp chilli flakes), 1 tbsp light soy sauce, 1 tbsp dark soy sauce, 10g honey, 1 tsp sesame oil, 250ml water
Slurry: 1 tbsp cornstarch + 2 tbsp cold water
1 tsp Sichuan peppercorns, crushed (or 1/2 tsp black pepper if not available)
instructions: Cook rice.
Heat oil in a wok or large pan, medium-high. Garlic and ginger, 1 min.
Add half the spring onion, Sichuan pepper, 30 seconds.
Stir in doubanjiang, cook 30 sec until oil reddens. Add soy sauces, honey, sesame oil, water. Simmer 2 min.
Gently add tofu cubes (don't smash). Simmer 4 min, gently stirring once or twice.
Stir cornstarch slurry, drizzle in. Simmer 1 min until thickened.
Plate over rice. Remaining spring onion and sesame over. Two.

name: Peanut Tofu Stir-Fry
description: Crispy tofu cubes in a peanut-soy sauce with stir-fried veg on rice.
ingredients: 300g extra-firm tofu, cubed and pressed (paper towel under a plate for 10 min)
Sauce: 25g peanut butter, 20g light soy sauce, 15g honey, 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tsp sriracha, 1 tsp sesame oil, 60ml water
Spring onion, crushed peanuts to finish
instructions: Cook rice.
Toss tofu with salt, garlic powder, cornstarch.
Sauce: whisk peanut butter, soy, honey, garlic, ginger, sriracha, sesame oil, water.
Heat half the oil in a pan, medium-high. Cook tofu 8 min, turning, until crispy on all sides. Remove.
Same pan, high: remaining oil. Add broccoli, capsicum, carrot. Stir-fry 4 min.
Pour in sauce, simmer 1 min. Return tofu, toss to coat.
Plate over rice. Spring onion and peanuts over. Two.

name: No-Bake Protein Balls
description: Peanut butter, oats, whey, honey. Roll, fridge, eat all week. One serve = 3 balls.
instructions: Mix oats, whey, peanut butter, honey, milk, vanilla, chocolate chips in a bowl.
Roll into balls (you'll get about 15).
Fridge 30 min to firm up.
Stored in an airtight container, lasts a week. One serve = 3 balls.

name: Turkey & Cheese Roll-Ups
description: Sliced turkey wrapped around light cheese. Zero cook, 24g protein, fits in your pocket.
ingredients: 100g sliced lean turkey breast (low-sodium if you can find it)
instructions: Lay turkey slices flat.
Spread thin smear of Dijon if using.
Top with cheese, roll up tight.
Black pepper, eat.

name: Tuna Cucumber Boats
description: Mashed tuna and yoghurt in cucumber halves. No bread needed.
ingredients: 1 large cucumber (200g), halved lengthwise and seeds scooped out
instructions: Drain tuna well. Mash with yoghurt, mayo, Dijon, chives, lemon, salt, pepper.
Spoon into the hollowed cucumber halves.
Eat with a fork or bite directly.

name: Cottage Cheese Veg Dip Pot
description: Blended cottage cheese into a creamy savoury dip with carrot, capsicum, cucumber.
instructions: Blend cottage cheese with lemon, garlic powder, dill, olive oil, salt, pepper until smooth and dip-like. (A small food processor or stick blender works best.)
Spoon into a container.
Cut veg into sticks.
Dip and eat. Sauce keeps 3 days in the fridge.

name: Baked Egg Muffins
description: Two mini frittata muffins. Batch cook 6, eat all week. Different to the breakfast version, bite-sized.
instructions: Oven 180°C / 360°F. Spray a muffin tin with oil.
Whisk eggs with salt, pepper, paprika.
Divide spinach, capsicum, spring onion, cheese between 4 muffin cups.
Pour egg mix over the top, fill each cup about 3/4.
Bake 16-18 min until set.
Cool. Stored in fridge 4 days. One serve = 2 muffins. Reheat 30 sec microwave or eat cold.

name: Cinnamon Apple & Peanut Butter
description: Sliced apple, peanut butter. Three ingredients counting the cinnamon. Toddler-tier easy, grown-up satisfying.
instructions: Slice apple into wedges.
Dip into peanut butter or smear on top. Cinnamon over.

name: Mini Chicken Meatballs
description: Batch-baked herby chicken meatballs. Eat cold from the fridge with a Greek yoghurt-herb dip. One serve = 4 meatballs.
ingredients: Dip: 60g 0% Greek yoghurt, 1 tsp dried herbs, squeeze of lemon, salt
instructions: Oven 200°C / 400°F. Line a tray.
Mix mince, parmesan, egg, breadcrumbs, garlic, salt, pepper, Italian herbs, smoked paprika in a bowl. Don't overwork.
Roll into 16 small meatballs.
Place on tray, brush with oil. Bake 16-18 min until browned and cooked through.
Cool. Stored in fridge 4 days.
Dip: mix all dip ingredients.
Serve = 4 meatballs + a spoon of dip. Eat cold or warm.

name: Soy Sesame Edamame
description: Edamame tossed in soy, sesame oil, chilli. More flavour than the plain version.
instructions: Boil water. Drop edamame in, 4-5 min. Drain.
In a bowl, toss with soy, sesame oil, chilli flakes, sesame seeds, salt.
Eat warm or cold. Suck the bean from the pod, discard pod.

name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Protein Shake
description: Cold post-training shake. Whey, milk, peanut butter, cocoa. Shake or blend.
instructions: Shaker: dry whey + milk first, shake hard, then add peanut butter & cocoa, shake again. Peanut butter will be slightly clumpy.
Blender (smoother): everything plus ice, blend 30 seconds.
Drink immediately.

name: Salmon & Avocado Rice Cake Stack
description: Smoked salmon, mashed avocado, light cream cheese on rice cakes. Five-minute fancy snack.
instructions: Mash avocado with lemon, salt, pepper.
Spread cream cheese on rice cakes. Top with mashed avocado.
Layer smoked salmon on top. Chilli flakes.

name: Chocolate Whipped Cottage Cheese
description: Blended cottage cheese with cocoa and honey, tastes like chocolate mousse, has 22g protein. Sounds wrong, eats right.
instructions: Blend cottage cheese, cocoa, honey, vanilla, salt until completely smooth, like mousse. A small food processor or stick blender works.
Spoon into a bowl.
Top with berries and chocolate chips.
Eat immediately or chill.

name: Tzatziki & Pita
description: Homemade cucumber-garlic yoghurt with warm pita. Mediterranean classic.
ingredients: 60g cucumber, grated and squeezed dry (this matters)
instructions: Grate cucumber, squeeze out as much water as you can with your hands or in a clean tea towel.
Mix yoghurt, cucumber, garlic, olive oil, lemon, dill, salt, pepper.
Warm pita in a dry pan 30 sec each side, or oven 1 min.
Cut pita into wedges. Dip and eat. Sauce keeps 3 days.

name: Berry Protein Smoothie
description: Frozen berries, banana, whey. Quick, sweet, 30g protein.
instructions: Blend everything 30-45 seconds until smooth.
Pour, drink.

name: Hard-Boiled Eggs & Avocado
description: Two boiled eggs, half an avocado, hot sauce. Boil six on Sunday, snack all week.
instructions: Eggs into cold water. Bring to boil, simmer 8-9 min. Cold water bath. Peel. (Do six at once on Sunday.)
Slice avocado.
Plate eggs and avocado. Salt, pepper, chilli flakes, lemon, hot sauce.

name: Greek Yogurt Protein Bowl
description: No cooking, three minutes, 40-plus grams of protein. The default high-protein breakfast when you are short on time.
instructions: Stir the whey through the yogurt until smooth.
Top with berries, granola and a drizzle of honey.

name: Egg White & Turkey Scramble
description: Lean, high-volume and barely any fat. A cutting-phase staple that still fills the plate.
instructions: Brown the turkey in the oil for 3-4 minutes.
Add spinach and tomatoes and cook until wilted.
Pour in the egg whites and scramble until just set.

name: Cottage Cheese & Berry Bowl
description: Slow-digesting casein from the cottage cheese keeps you full all morning. Whey lifts the protein further.
instructions: Stir the whey into the cottage cheese until smooth.
Top with berries and honey, dust with cinnamon if you like.

name: Protein Pancakes
description: Light, fluffy and built almost entirely from protein and a banana. A weekend-feel breakfast that fits the macros.
instructions: Blend everything into a smooth batter.
Cook small pancakes in a nonstick pan, 1-2 minutes a side.
Stack and top with sugar-free syrup or a spoon of yogurt.

name: Banana Peanut Protein Smoothie
description: Breakfast you can drink on the way out the door. Blends in under two minutes.
instructions: Add everything to a blender with a handful of ice.
Blend until smooth and pour.

name: Overnight Protein Oats
description: Mix it the night before and breakfast makes itself. Thick, creamy and ready in the fridge.
instructions: Stir oats, milk, whey and chia in a jar.
Cover and chill overnight.
Top with blueberries before eating.

name: Savoury Egg White Oats
description: Don't knock it until you try it. Oats go creamy and savoury, parmesan does the flavour, egg whites do the protein.
instructions: Cook the oats in water until thick.
Stir in the egg whites off the heat until creamy.
Season well and finish with parmesan.

name: Scrambled Eggs on Toast
description: The classic done with extra whites to push the protein without piling on fat.
instructions: Whisk the eggs and whites with salt and pepper.
Scramble gently in the oil over low heat until just set.
Serve on toasted wholegrain bread.

name: Avocado & Cottage Cheese Toast
description: Cottage cheese carries the protein so the avocado can stay for the good fats and the flavour.
instructions: Toast the bread.
Spread cottage cheese, then smashed avocado.
Top with tomatoes, salt, pepper and chilli flakes.

name: Egg & Veg Mug Omelette
description: No stove needed. Whisk, microwave, eat. Perfect for a dorm, an office or a campervan.
instructions: Whisk everything in a large mug.
Microwave 90 seconds, stir, then 30-45 seconds more until set.

name: Mocha Protein Shake
description: Caffeine and 45-plus grams of protein in one glass. Fuel and a coffee hit at the same time.
instructions: Blend everything with ice until smooth and frothy.

name: Greek Yogurt Granola Parfait
description: Layered for texture, with nut butter and granola adding the carbs and fats to fuel a session.
instructions: Stir whey into the yogurt.
Layer with granola in a glass and swirl through the nut butter.

name: Tuna & Egg White Wrap
description: Savoury, filling and almost pure protein. Yes, tuna for breakfast works.
instructions: Scramble the egg whites in a nonstick pan.
Mix the tuna with mustard.
Fill the wrap with egg, tuna and spinach and roll up.

name: Turkey & Egg Muffin Stack
description: A clean take on the drive-thru muffin. Toasted, stacked and ready in eight minutes.
ingredients: 50g lean turkey mince, as a patty
instructions: Toast the muffin.
Cook the turkey patty and the egg in a pan.
Stack patty, egg and cheese inside the muffin.

name: Smoked Salmon Bagel Thin
description: No cooking, omega-3s and a proper protein hit. Eats like a treat, fits a cut.
instructions: Toast the bagel thin.
Spread with cottage cheese.
Layer over smoked salmon and cucumber, finish with pepper and lemon.

name: Chia Protein Pudding
description: Set it overnight and it turns thick and spoonable. Quiet, slow-release protein and a load of fibre.
instructions: Whisk milk, whey and chia.
Chill overnight until set.
Top with berries.

name: Apple Cinnamon Protein Oats
description: Warm, sweet and spiced like apple pie, without the sugar load. A cosy training-day breakfast.
instructions: Cook oats in the milk until thick.
Stir in whey off the heat with plenty of cinnamon.
Top with diced apple.

name: Cheesy Veggie Egg Muffins
description: Bake a tray on Sunday and grab two a morning all week. Genuinely the easiest meal prep there is.
instructions: Heat oven to 180C.
Whisk eggs and whites, stir in veg and cheese, season.
Pour into a greased muffin tin and bake 18 minutes. Makes 6; two muffins is a serving.

name: Peanut Butter Banana Protein Toast
description: Carbs, protein and good fats stacked on toast. Five minutes, eats like dessert.
ingredients: 15g vanilla whey mixed to a paste
instructions: Toast the bread.
Spread peanut butter, then a thin whey-and-water paste.
Top with banana slices.

name: Skyr Power Bowl
description: Skyr is thick, tart and very high protein. Granola and almonds round it into a full breakfast.
instructions: Spoon skyr into a bowl.
Top with granola, blueberries and almonds.

name: Chicken & Rice Power Bowl
description: The original gym lunch. Lean chicken, rice and greens that reheats perfectly all week.
instructions: Season and pan-cook the chicken, or use pre-cooked.
Layer rice, chicken and green beans in a container.
Drizzle with teriyaki and a little oil.

name: Buffalo Chicken Rice Bowl
description: Creamy, spicy and built for meal prep. A bowl you won't get bored of by Thursday.
instructions: Toss the shredded chicken in hot sauce.
Whisk yogurt into a quick ranch-style dressing.
Serve over rice with the veg and dressing.

name: Quick Teriyaki Salmon Rice Bowl
description: Pre-cooked salmon and a rice pouch turn into a five-minute bowl with serious omega-3s.
instructions: Warm the rice and edamame.
Flake the salmon on top.
Drizzle teriyaki and scatter sesame seeds.

name: Chicken Burrito Bowl
description: All the flavour of a burrito with none of the fuss. Loads of protein and fibre.
instructions: Warm chicken, rice and beans.
Build the bowl and top with salsa and cheese.

name: Tuna Pasta Salad
description: A cold-prep desk lunch that travels well and stays high in protein.
instructions: Boil the pasta and cool it.
Fold through tuna, sweetcorn, tomatoes and light mayo.
Season and chill.

name: Chicken Caesar Wrap
description: The takeaway favourite, rebuilt lean with a yogurt-based caesar.
instructions: Slice the chicken.
Toss lettuce with the yogurt caesar and parmesan.
Fill the wrap and roll tightly.

name: Chicken & Hummus Wrap
description: No cooking needed. Hummus does the job of mayo with more fibre and flavour.
instructions: Spread hummus on the wrap.
Add chicken, spinach and carrot, then roll.

name: Turkey Taco Rice Bowl
description: Seasoned turkey mince over rice and beans. Cheap, fast and reliably tasty.
instructions: Brown the turkey with taco seasoning.
Pile over rice and beans.
Top with salsa and cheese.

name: Chicken Couscous Salad
description: Instant couscous and pre-cooked chicken make a bright lunch you can eat warm or cold.
instructions: Soak the couscous in boiling water for five minutes and fluff.
Fold in chopped chicken and veg.
Dress with oil, lemon and herbs.

name: Smoked Salmon Quinoa Bowl
description: A five-minute assembly bowl with quinoa, smoked salmon and avocado. Light but very filling.
instructions: Spoon quinoa into a bowl.
Top with salmon, avocado and cucumber.
Finish with a lemon-yogurt drizzle.

name: Tuna Cottage Cheese Crackers
description: A no-cook lunch that is almost all protein. Keeps you sharp through the afternoon.
instructions: Mix the tuna with the cottage cheese, pepper and lemon.
Spoon onto the crackers.

name: Chicken Pesto Pasta
description: Pesto brings big flavour for little effort. A bowl that feels like a treat but hits the numbers.
instructions: Boil the pasta.
Toss with chicken, pesto, tomatoes and spinach until the spinach wilts.

name: Beef & Broccoli Rice Bowl
description: Lean mince, broccoli and a soy glaze over rice. A takeaway-style bowl that meal-preps well.
instructions: Brown the mince and steam the broccoli.
Toss with soy sauce and serve over rice.

name: Prawn Rice Bowl
description: Prawns cook in minutes and are nearly all protein. A light, fresh bowl for warmer days.
instructions: Warm the rice and prawns.
Build the bowl with edamame and cucumber.
Drizzle with sriracha.

name: Chicken Quesadilla
description: Pan-toasted and ready fast. Use pre-cooked chicken and it eats like a treat.
instructions: Fill half the tortilla with chicken, cheese and salsa.
Fold and toast in a dry pan two minutes a side.
Slice into wedges.

name: Lentil & Feta Rice Bowl
description: A vegetarian bowl with real staying power from lentils, finished with salty feta.
instructions: Warm the lentils and rice.
Stir spinach through to wilt.
Crumble over feta and finish with oil and lemon.

name: Chicken Egg Fried Rice
description: Day-old rice, a couple of eggs and pre-cooked chicken. Better and leaner than the takeaway.
instructions: Scramble the eggs and set aside.
Fry rice, chicken and veg in a hot pan.
Fold the egg back in with soy sauce.

name: Mediterranean Chicken Quinoa Bowl
description: Bright, herby and built for cold prep. Feta and lemon lift simple chicken and quinoa.
instructions: Combine quinoa, chicken and chopped veg.
Crumble over feta.
Dress with lemon, oregano and pepper.

name: Turkey Meatball Marinara Bowl
description: Lean turkey meatballs in marinara over pasta. Comfort food that stays on plan.
instructions: Roll and pan-cook the turkey meatballs.
Simmer in marinara.
Serve over pasta with wilted spinach.

name: Teriyaki Tofu Rice Bowl
description: A plant-based bowl with crisp tofu and a sticky glaze. Holds up well as a packed lunch.
instructions: Pan-fry the tofu until golden.
Toss in teriyaki.
Serve over rice with edamame.

name: One-Pan Chicken & Veg with Rice
description: Everything in one pan, minimal washing up, solid macros. A weeknight default.
instructions: Cook the chicken in the oil.
Add veg and cook through.
Stir in rice and soy until hot.

name: Lean Beef Tacos
description: Brown, season, fill. A fast family-style dinner that lands right on the numbers.
instructions: Brown the mince with taco seasoning.
Warm the tortillas.
Fill with beef, cheese, salsa and lettuce.

name: Teriyaki Chicken Stir-Fry
description: Sticky, sweet and faster than delivery. Loads of veg and a clean protein hit.
instructions: Stir-fry the chicken in a hot pan.
Add veg for a couple of minutes.
Toss in teriyaki and serve over rice.

name: Salmon & Potato Traybake
description: One tray, almost no effort. Roast salmon, potatoes and greens together while you rest.
instructions: Heat oven to 200C.
Roast potatoes 15 minutes, then add salmon and broccoli.
Roast 12 minutes more, finish with lemon. Serves two.

name: Turkey Meatballs & Pasta
description: Lean meatballs simmered in marinara over pasta. 40-plus grams of protein, big comfort.
instructions: Roll and brown the meatballs.
Simmer in marinara while the pasta boils.
Combine and finish with parmesan.

name: Chicken Fajitas
description: Sizzling peppers, onions and chicken folded into warm tortillas. A crowd-pleaser that's still lean.
instructions: Fry the chicken with fajita seasoning.
Add peppers and onion until charred.
Serve in warm tortillas.

name: Lean Smash Burgers
description: Thin, crispy-edged patties from lean mince. A burger night that fits a cut.
instructions: Smash the mince into thin patties in a hot pan.
Sear hard both sides and melt the cheese.
Build in the toasted bun.

name: Beef & Broccoli Stir-Fry
description: A takeaway classic, leaner at home. Lean strips, broccoli and a glossy soy-garlic sauce.
instructions: Sear the beef in the oil.
Add broccoli and a splash of water to steam.
Toss in soy and garlic, serve over rice.

name: Baked Cod & Sweet Potato Mash
description: Flaky white fish over sweet potato mash. Very lean, very filling, easy on the stomach.
instructions: Bake the cod 15 minutes.
Boil and mash the sweet potato.
Serve with steamed green beans.

name: Light Chicken Pasta Bake
description: Creamy without the cream, thanks to a yogurt-and-cheese sauce. Bake once, eat twice.
instructions: Boil the pasta.
Mix with chicken, marinara and yogurt.
Top with cheese and bake 15 minutes. Serves two.

name: Honey Garlic Chicken & Rice
description: Sweet, sticky and quick. A glaze of honey, garlic and soy over lean chicken.
instructions: Pan-cook the chicken.
Add honey, soy and garlic to glaze.
Serve over rice with steamed broccoli.

name: Sweet Chilli Salmon Noodles
description: Salmon flaked through noodles with a sweet-chilli kick. Restaurant flavour, fifteen minutes.
instructions: Cook the noodles.
Stir-fry the veg, then add flaked salmon.
Toss with noodles and sweet chilli.

name: Turkey Chili
description: A one-pot chili loaded with beans and lean turkey. Even better the next day.
instructions: Brown the turkey with onion and peppers.
Add beans, tomatoes and chili spices.
Simmer 15 minutes. Serves two.

name: Chicken Souvlaki Bowl
description: Greek-style chicken over rice with cool tzatziki. Big flavour from a short ingredient list.
instructions: Marinate and pan-cook the chicken with oregano and lemon.
Serve over rice.
Top with tzatziki and chopped salad.

name: Garlic Parmesan Chicken Pasta
description: Comfort-food pasta made lean. Roasted garlic and parmesan do the heavy lifting.
instructions: Boil the pasta.
Stir yogurt, parmesan and garlic into a quick sauce off the heat.
Fold through chicken, pasta and spinach.

name: Tofu Veg Stir-Fry
description: Crisp tofu and plenty of veg in a soy-ginger sauce. A satisfying plant-based plate.
instructions: Pan-fry the tofu until crisp.
Add veg and stir-fry briefly.
Toss with soy and ginger, serve over rice.

name: Halloumi & Chickpea Traybake
description: A vegetarian tray of roasted chickpeas, peppers and golden halloumi. Hearty and protein-rich.
instructions: Heat oven to 200C.
Roast chickpeas, peppers and onion 15 minutes.
Add sliced halloumi and roast 8 minutes more. Serves two.

name: Chicken Sausage & Veg Tray
description: Lean chicken sausages roasted with potatoes and peppers. Set the timer and walk away.
instructions: Heat oven to 200C.
Toss everything with oil on a tray.
Roast 18 minutes until golden.

name: Prawn Stir-Fry Rice
description: Prawns and veg tossed through fried rice. Light, quick and high in lean protein.
instructions: Scramble the egg and set aside.
Fry rice, prawns and veg.
Fold the egg back in with soy sauce.

name: Steak & Garlic Green Beans
description: A lean steak seared simply, with garlicky green beans and a baked potato. Date-night easy.
instructions: Sear the steak to your liking and rest it.
Saute green beans with garlic in the oil.
Serve with a baked potato.

name: Greek Yogurt, Honey & Berries
description: The simplest 20-gram protein hit there is. Thick yogurt, a little honey, a handful of berries.
instructions: Spoon yogurt into a pot.
Top with berries and honey.

name: Pineapple Cottage Cheese Cup
description: A retro gym snack for a reason: high protein, sweet, and it travels in a tub.
instructions: Combine the cottage cheese and pineapple in a tub.

name: Chocolate Protein Shake
description: Mixed in thirty seconds. A reliable way to land protein between meals.
instructions: Shake the whey and milk together until smooth.

name: Whipped Cottage Cheese & PB
description: Blend cottage cheese smooth and it eats like a mousse. Peanut butter makes it feel indulgent.
instructions: Blend the cottage cheese until smooth.
Swirl through peanut butter and honey.

name: Skyr Chocolate Mousse
description: Cocoa whisked into thick skyr. A lean dessert that quietly hits 24 grams of protein.
instructions: Whisk cocoa and honey into the skyr until smooth and thick.

name: Edamame & Soy Pot
description: Shelled edamame with a splash of soy. Plant protein and fibre in one warm pot.
instructions: Microwave the edamame for three minutes.
Toss with soy and chilli flakes.

name: Beef Jerky & Apple
description: Pure portable protein with a crisp apple alongside. Nothing to prepare, nothing to chill.
instructions: Pack the jerky and apple together. Eat on the go.

name: Rice Cakes, PB & Banana
description: Quick carbs and a little protein for around a session. Light but satisfying.
instructions: Spread peanut butter on the rice cakes.
Top with banana slices.

name: No-Bake Protein Oat Bars
description: Press a tray, chill, and cut into bars. Cheaper and cleaner than the shop versions.
instructions: Mix everything into a stiff paste.
Press into a small dish and chill 30 minutes.
Cut into two bars.

name: Boiled Eggs & Fruit
description: Two eggs and a piece of fruit. Old-school, portable, and naturally high protein.
instructions: Boil the eggs 8-9 minutes and cool.
Eat with the apple.

name: Tuna Pouch & Crackers
description: Peel a tuna pouch, grab some crackers, done. Savoury protein with zero cooking.
instructions: Open the tuna pouch.
Scoop onto the crackers with pepper and lemon.

name: Protein Yogurt Ranch & Veg
description: A high-protein dip that turns crunchy veg into a proper snack.
instructions: Stir ranch or onion seasoning into the yogurt.
Dip the veg sticks.

name: Apple & Peanut Butter
description: Crisp apple and a scoop of peanut butter. Not the highest protein, but a clean, satisfying fix.
instructions: Slice the apple.
Serve with peanut butter for dipping.

name: Chocolate Peanut Protein Balls
description: Roll a batch and keep them in the fridge. Two or three is a perfect protein-and-carb snack.
instructions: Mix into a thick dough.
Roll into balls and chill. Half the batch is one serving.

name: Savoury Cottage Cheese Toast
description: One slice, loaded with cottage cheese. A fast, savoury bridge between meals.
instructions: Toast the bread.
Top with cottage cheese, tomatoes, salt and pepper.

name: Protein Iced Coffee
description: Cold coffee blended with protein. Caffeine and 25-plus grams of protein in one glass.
instructions: Shake or blend everything with ice.

name: Nuts & Skyr
description: Thick skyr with a small scatter of nuts for crunch and healthy fats.
instructions: Top the skyr with chopped nuts.

name: Cottage Cheese Berry Bowl
description: A lighter cousin of the breakfast bowl, sized as a snack but just as high in protein.
instructions: Spoon berries over the cottage cheese.

name: Frozen Greek Yogurt Bark
description: Spread, freeze, snap into shards. A frozen treat that is really just protein and fruit.
instructions: Stir honey into the yogurt and spread thin on a lined tray.
Scatter berries and freeze two hours.
Snap into pieces. Half is a serving.

name: Peanut Banana Protein Smoothie
description: A smaller smoothie sized as a snack. Banana, peanut butter and whey blended cold.
instructions: Blend everything with ice until smooth.

name: Vegan Peanut Butter Oats
description: Warm oats with banana and peanut butter, made on soy milk for a fully plant-based start with real staying power.
instructions: Simmer the oats in soy milk for 3-4 minutes, stirring, until creamy.
Stir through the peanut butter and chia.
Top with the sliced banana.

name: Vegan Berry Overnight Oats
description: Mix it the night before and it is ready when you are. Berries and chia keep it fresh and high in fibre.
instructions: Stir the oats, soy milk, chia and maple syrup in a jar.
Chill overnight.
Top with the berries before eating.

name: Vegan Protein Shake
description: A blended plant-protein shake with banana and oats for a fast, drinkable breakfast that still hits the protein.
instructions: Add everything to a blender.
Blend until smooth, loosen with water or ice if needed.

name: Tofu Scramble Wrap
description: A savoury scramble of crumbled tofu and peppers folded into a warm wrap. Turmeric gives it that egg-like colour.
instructions: Fry the tofu in the oil with a pinch of turmeric for 4-5 minutes.
Add pepper and spinach until softened.
Spoon into the wrap with salsa and roll up.

name: Smashed Chickpea Wrap
description: Chickpeas mashed with avocado make a creamy, no-cook filling that holds up in a lunchbox all day.
instructions: Roughly mash the chickpeas with the avocado and a squeeze of lemon.
Season well.
Spread into the wrap with spinach and sweetcorn, then roll.

name: Quinoa Black Bean Bowl
description: A cold or warm bowl loaded with plant protein and fibre. Salsa and avocado do the flavour work.
instructions: Warm the quinoa and beans if you like, or keep it cold.
Build the bowl and top with salsa and sliced avocado.

name: Crispy Tofu Burrito Bowl
description: Pan-crisped tofu over rice and beans with salsa and avocado. A burrito bowl that happens to be vegan.
instructions: Fry the tofu cubes until golden on all sides.
Layer rice, beans and tofu.
Top with salsa and avocado.

name: Falafel & Hummus Bowl
description: Shop-bought falafel turns a few salad bits into a Mediterranean bowl in minutes. Big on flavour and fibre.
instructions: Soak the couscous in boiling water for 5 minutes, then fluff.
Warm the falafel.
Build the bowl with couscous, salad and a spoon of hummus.

name: BBQ Chicken Rice Bowl
description: Smoky barbecue chicken over rice with corn and beans. A sweet-savoury prep bowl that keeps well.
instructions: Toss the chicken in the barbecue sauce.
Layer over rice with the corn and beans.
Warm through and serve.

name: Honey Mustard Chicken Wrap
description: A creamy honey-mustard chicken wrap using yogurt instead of mayo, so it stays high in protein.
instructions: Mix the yogurt, honey and a little mustard into a dressing.
Toss with the sliced chicken.
Fill the wrap with chicken and spinach, then roll.

name: Tuna Sweetcorn Jacket Potato
description: The classic cafe jacket potato, rebuilt lean. A microwave potato and a tin of tuna and you are done.
instructions: Microwave the potato until soft, 8-10 minutes, then crisp in a hot oven if you have time.
Mix the tuna, sweetcorn and light mayo.
Split the potato and pile the filling in.

name: Turkey & Avocado Rice Bowl
description: Lean turkey mince over rice with salsa and avocado. A quick taco-style bowl that reheats cleanly.
instructions: Brown the turkey mince with a pinch of cumin and paprika.
Serve over rice with the pepper.
Top with salsa and avocado.

name: Black Bean & Sweet Potato Chilli
description: A thick, smoky chilli built on beans and sweet potato. Makes two big bowls and tastes better the next day.
instructions: Soften the onion and pepper in a pot.
Add sweet potato, beans, corn and marinara with chilli and cumin.
Simmer 15-18 minutes until the sweet potato is tender.

name: Tempeh Teriyaki Bowl
description: Sliced tempeh glazed in teriyaki over rice with broccoli and edamame. High in plant protein and seriously moreish.
instructions: Pan-fry the tempeh until browned.
Add the teriyaki and let it glaze.
Serve over rice with steamed broccoli and edamame.

name: Chickpea Coconut Curry
description: A mild, creamy chickpea curry in light coconut milk. One pot, big flavour, and plenty of fibre.
instructions: Soften the onion and pepper, then stir in curry powder.
Add chickpeas and coconut milk and simmer 12-15 minutes.
Wilt in the spinach and serve over rice.

name: Roasted Chilli Chickpeas
description: Crunchy roasted chickpeas with a little heat. A high-fibre, plant-protein snack to keep at your desk.
instructions: Toss the chickpeas with the oil, paprika and a pinch of chilli.
Roast at 200C for 18-20 minutes until crisp.

name: Maple Cinnamon Almonds
description: Almonds with a touch of maple and cinnamon. Healthy fats and a little sweetness without the sugar crash.
instructions: Toss the almonds with the maple syrup and cinnamon.
Eat as is, or bake 5 minutes for a candied finish.

name: Sausage, Egg & Cheese Muffin
description: The drive-thru classic, made lean at home. A toasted muffin stacked with egg, chicken sausage and melted cheese.
instructions: Cook the sausage patty and fry the eggs to your liking.
Toast the muffin and add the cheese to melt.
Stack and serve hot.

name: Cinnamon Roll Protein Oats
description: Tastes like a cinnamon roll, built like a protein breakfast. Creamy oats with a vanilla protein swirl.
instructions: Cook the oats in the milk with plenty of cinnamon.
Off the heat, stir in the whey until creamy.
Finish with a swirl of maple syrup.

name: Ham & Cheese Omelette
description: A diner-style folded omelette loaded with lean ham, cheese and peppers. Fast, filling and high in protein.
instructions: Beat the eggs and pour into a hot nonstick pan.
Scatter ham, pepper and cheese over one half.
Fold and slide out once just set.

name: Chicken Bacon Ranch Wrap
description: One of the most-loved wraps going. Chicken, a little crispy bacon and a creamy yogurt ranch, rolled and ready.
instructions: Stir the yogurt with a little garlic, onion and herbs for a quick ranch.
Toss the chicken in the ranch.
Fill the wrap with chicken, bacon, cheese and lettuce, then roll.

name: Philly Cheesesteak Bowl
description: All the flavour of a cheesesteak over rice instead of a roll. Steak, peppers, onions and melty cheese.
instructions: Sear the steak strips, then set aside.
Soften the peppers and onion in the same pan.
Return the steak, melt the cheese over the top and serve on rice.

name: Sweet & Sour Chicken Rice Bowl
description: The takeaway favourite, lightened up. Tender chicken and pineapple in a sticky sweet-and-sour glaze.
instructions: Warm the chicken with the peppers and pineapple.
Stir through the sweet and sour sauce until sticky.
Serve over rice.

name: Chicken Shawarma Bowl
description: Spiced shawarma-style chicken over rice with a cool garlic yogurt. A massive flavour hit for meal prep.
instructions: Toss the chicken with cumin, paprika, garlic and a squeeze of lemon, then warm through.
Build the bowl over rice with the salad.
Spoon the tzatziki on top.

name: Salmon Poke-Style Bowl
description: The viral poke bowl, no raw fish needed if you use cooked salmon. Fresh, light and full of omega-3s.
instructions: Cool the rice slightly and add a splash of soy and sriracha.
Top with salmon, edamame, avocado and cucumber.
Finish with sesame seeds.

name: Beef Burrito Bowl
description: Seasoned beef over rice and beans with salsa and cheese. The beef answer to the chicken burrito bowl.
instructions: Season the mince with cumin, paprika and a little chilli.
Layer rice, beans and beef.
Top with salsa and cheese.

name: Tuna Melt
description: The classic cafe tuna melt, high in protein and ready in minutes. Crispy bread, creamy tuna, melted cheese.
instructions: Mix the tuna with the mayo.
Pile onto the bread and top with cheese.
Grill or pan-toast until the cheese melts.

name: Chicken Parm-Style Pasta
description: A lean take on chicken parm, the comfort-food legend. Crisp chicken, marinara and melted mozzarella over pasta.
instructions: Boil the pasta.
Top the chicken with marinara and mozzarella and grill until bubbling.
Serve over the pasta with the rest of the sauce.

name: Spaghetti Bolognese
description: The everyday classic, done lean. A rich beef and tomato sauce over spaghetti that everyone already loves.
instructions: Soften the onion, then brown the mince.
Add the marinara and simmer 10-12 minutes.
Serve over cooked spaghetti.

name: Butter Chicken with Rice
description: A milder, creamy curry-house favourite. Tender chicken in a tomato and yogurt sauce over rice.
instructions: Warm the sauce and stir in the yogurt for creaminess.
Add the chicken and simmer 8-10 minutes.
Serve over rice.

name: Cajun Tomato Chicken & Sausage Pasta
description: A smoky, spicy one-pan pasta that tastes far more indulgent than the macros suggest.
instructions: Boil the pasta.
Brown the chicken and sausage with cajun seasoning and peppers.
Stir in the marinara and pasta and toss to coat.

name: Honey Sriracha Chicken Bowl
description: Sweet, spicy and sticky. A glazed chicken bowl that hits the same note as your favourite takeaway.
instructions: Whisk the honey and sriracha into a glaze.
Toss the chicken in the glaze and warm through.
Serve over rice with steamed broccoli.

name: Shrimp Tacos
description: Quick prawn tacos with salsa and avocado. Light, fresh and on the table in under 20 minutes.
instructions: Warm the prawns with a little chilli and lime.
Heat the tortillas.
Fill with prawns, salsa, avocado and a little cheese.

name: Cheesy Chicken & Salsa Bowl
description: Saucy salsa chicken over rice and beans, finished with melted cheese. Big Mexican-style comfort in a bowl.
instructions: Warm the chicken in the salsa with a little cumin.
Layer over rice and beans.
Top with cheese and grill briefly to melt.

name: Lemon Pepper Chicken & Potatoes
description: A simple, big-plate roast dinner. Zesty lemon-pepper chicken with potatoes and greens, all on one tray.
instructions: Toss the potatoes in oil and roast until golden.
Season the chicken with lemon zest and cracked pepper and roast through.
Steam the green beans and serve alongside.

name: Protein Cookie Dough
description: An edible cookie-dough that is actually a protein snack. No baking, ready in five minutes.
instructions: Mix everything into a thick dough, loosening with milk as needed.
Fold in a few dark chocolate chips if you like.
Chill briefly and eat with a spoon.

name: Cheesecake Cottage Cheese Bowl
description: Blended cottage cheese tastes shockingly like cheesecake filling. A high-protein dessert-style snack.
instructions: Blend the cottage cheese and whey until smooth and creamy.
Top with berries and granola for crunch.

name: Banana Protein Shake
description: Fast protein hit. Shake or blend, done in a minute.
instructions: Add the whey, milk, and banana to a blender or shaker.
Blend or shake until smooth. Drink.

name: Crumpets & Jam
description: A simple carb-heavy snack. Toast, butter, jam.
instructions: Toast the crumpets.
Spread with butter, then jam. Eat warm.
